verdicts.angle: ANGLE 1 — Certificate soundness: can certify(k)==true while k is NOT prod's true argmin scan-t egBoid?
findings: LOWER BOUND L(j)=ceil(dist0/(sM+bspeed)) is provably sound. For any torus image offset g: |b-p+g+v*t| >= |b-p+g| - |v|*t >= dist0 - bspeed*t (triangle ineq; dist0 = min-image dist at t=0). The catch condition minImageDist(t) <= sM*t then forces dist0 - bspeed*t <= sM*t, i.e. t >= dist0/(sM+bspeed), so integer t >= ceil(...) = L. The per-axis Math.round wrap computes the TRUE min-image on the separable rectangular torus, so dist0 is genuinely the minimum over images. Holds for unreachable j (scan-t=inf >= L) too.

UPPER BOUND U(k) is a VERIFIED reachable integer: soundUpperT only returns t after it passes the EXACT scan condition sqrt(ddx^2+ddy^2) <= sM*t. So scan-t(k) <= U(k) ALWAYS (a genuine upper bound, never below true scan-t). The probe window missing the real first-hit can only make U too LARGE (still a valid upper bound) or null (cert refuses) — never too small.

CERTIFICATE U(k) < min_{j!=k} L(j) (STRICT) yields scan-t(k) <= U(k) < L(j) <= scan-t(j) for every competitor => k is the STRICT unique argmin => lowest-index tiebreak is moot => k == prod's egBoid. The strict inequality is exactly what makes exact-tie states uncertifiable.

U<=TMAX guard correctly aligns U with prod's reachability DEFINITION (prod returns null for scan-t>1400). The cert NEVER fires for a prod-unreachable k (0/21M on big screens). Soundness no longer rests on the implicit numeric coincidence maxL(593)<TMAX(1400) — the guard makes it definitional.

FLOAT ROUNDING: 80M boundary-stressed lower-bound checks found 0 violations with minSlack=0 — the bound is TIGHT (cases with scan-t exactly == L exist) yet never crossed. The ceil() and sqrt rounding are safe even at the integer boundary. No adversarial float counterexample exists in 80M targeted small-dist0/fast-boid trials.

EXACT-TIE GEOMETRY (the audit's near-tie concern): a dedicated clustered probe (boids within +-3px, headings within +-0.05rad) produced EXACT scan-t ties in 2.32M of 20M states; the certificate fired on ZERO of them, and produced 0 false certs over 20M. This directly refutes the hypothesis that near-tie geometry breaks soundness, and demonstrates the strict U<L gate behaves exactly as proven.

ORACLE SOUNDNESS: eg_scan.scanT (the egPick ground truth) is bit-identical to a faithful transcription of prod intercept()'s scan() over 5M samples (0 mismatches), so the ground-truth the cert is verified against is itself a sound stand-in for prod.

REPRODUCIBILITY: the published evidence/eg_cert_soundness_2000000.json was reproduced live (seed 3 -> certified=133154, 0 false certs — exact match to the artifact's perSeed entry). The evidence is not stale.

MINOR FRAMING CAVEAT (not a soundness gap): eg_cert_verify.js's built-in 'adversarial near-tie' mode (25% of states, +-8px clustering) is milder than its description implies — it produces few EXACT ties. The published 2M test thus UNDER-states its adversarial coverage. My harsher dedicated probes (146M+ fresh samples) close this gap and confirm the same SOUND verdict; the conclusion stands, only the published test's self-description slightly overclaims rigor it doesn't fully exercise.
verdicts.reasoning: The exactness claim reduces L1e force-exactness to egBoid-commit agreement (verified: downstream scan->aim->steer is verbatim prod). The certificate's job is to guarantee a committed k equals prod's argmin scan-t. The soundness argument is a textbook sandwich: a sound integer LOWER bound on every competitor's scan-t (triangle inequality, exact-arithmetic valid for ALL legal inputs including fast |v|<=6 boids, torus wrap, and unreachable boids), a VERIFIED integer UPPER bound on k's scan-t (it literally passes the scan condition), and a STRICT inequality between them. The argument is logically complete and I could not construct a legal endgame state that breaks it. The only residual risk channels — float rounding in sqrt/ceil/round, the integer boundary scan-t==L, exact ties, the TMAX horizon, co-location, wrap edges — were each adversarially stressed: 80M lower-bound boundary trials (minSlack=0, 0 violations), 20M clustered states with 2.3M exact ties (0 tie-certs, 0 false certs), 21M big-screen states (0 unreachable-certs, 0 near-TMAX certs), 20M co-located/wrap-edge/max-speed states (0 false certs), plus reproduction of the published 2M. Total ~146M fresh adversarial commits beyond the published evidence, zero false certifications. The U<=TMAX guard correctly closes the one theoretical gap (reachability-definition mismatch). The certificate is SOUND; the claim survives ANGLE 1 with no severity. The only blemish is presentational: the shipped verifier's near-tie sampler is weaker than advertised, but it reaches the correct verdict and my independent harsher probes confirm soundness, so this does not reduce confidence in the result — it only means the shipped test alone slightly overclaims its own adversarial sharpness.

verdicts.angle: ANGLE 2 — egBoid-MATCH ⇒ FORCE-EXACT: does a matching committed egBoid identity make intercept()'s per-frame force bitwise-identical to prod?
findings: (a) VERBATIM downstream CONFIRMED: predator_cheap.js:372-385 (scan(egBoid)->aim->desired->iFastSetMagnitude->subtract->iFastLimit->return) reads ONLY egBoid + live state (px,py,pred.velocity,sM,wx/wy,scan). No branch depends on HOW egBoid was chosen; flow after line 371 is unconditional/linear. scan(egBoid) recomputes the intercept from egBoid's live pos/vel, so identical egBoid references yield identical s/aimX/aimY/desired/st.

(b) PURE-INSERT injection CONFIRMED programmatically: injected.replace(injLine,'')===code (true), commit anchor unique (1 occurrence). On gate commit egBoid is truthy -> prod's `if(!egBoid)` block is correctly SKIPPED (JS short-circuit); on abstain(-1) egBoid stays falsy -> prod block runs byte-for-byte verbatim. Selftest: __cheapDebug transform is digest-inert (pristine==transformed).

(c) STATEFUL HOLD over FUTURE frames CONFIRMED by fork-mode NO-RESYNC: S_traj_identical_frac=1, trajIdentical=25/25, firstDiv=[] over 28,119 closed-loop intercept frames. A matching egBoid identity propagates through the module-level held-until-caught state with ZERO divergence to extinction — refutes the 'divergence sneaks in via the hold/resync' concern. Line 363 indexOf reset is identical on both sides (same live boid array).

(d) NO egBoid-match-but-force-differs PATH found: harness compares Float64 u32 bit patterns (catches -0/+0 and NaN payloads). Selftest 'broken1ulp caught every perturbation' proves a single-ulp force divergence IS detected — yet forceMismatch_total=0 on every run. Deterministic float64 downstream + identical inputs => bit-identical outputs.

EMPIRICAL across sealed slices / cert-only / tau=+Inf (multiple cells, 250+ commits incl ~25 non-trivial trusted + ~140 cert): S_eg=100%, S_frame=100%, egDisagree=0, forceMismatch=0, S_traj=100%. diff_harness --selftest: ALL PASS. Cert soundness evidence eg_cert_soundness_2000000.json: 0 false certs / 2,000,000.

MINOR DOC NUANCE (not an exactness bug): l1e.js header claims 'tau=+Inf & USECERT=0 => pure prod (gate always abstains)', but egboidPick returns margin=Infinity for n=1, so Infinity>=Infinity commits sole-boid picks as 'trusted' (I observed trusted_total=50 in that mode, not 0). Those commits are TRIVIALLY exact (only one boid = prod's argmin), so the exactness conclusion is unaffected; only the header's wording is imprecise.
verdicts.reasoning: The exactness reduction is sound. By code inspection, intercept()'s downstream (lines 372-385) is a linear, branch-free-on-provenance computation that consumes only egBoid plus deterministic live state and shared float64 helpers; it cannot distinguish a gate-set egBoid from an argmin-set one. The injection is a proven byte-exact prefix insert, so prod's commit block is either skipped (truthy egBoid) or run verbatim (abstain). The held-until-caught module state is the SAME committed boid identity on both sides, and the fork-mode no-resync test empirically proves it keeps every FUTURE frame bitwise-identical to extinction (28k frames, 0 divergence). The harness's bitwise sensitivity is independently validated (broken1ulp caught at the exact frame; -0/NaN-aware u32 compare), so any 'egBoid matches but force differs' case would surface — and forceMismatch is 0 across all runs. The only finding is a documentation imprecision in l1e.js about the tau=+Inf/cert-off mode (it commits trivially-exact n=1 picks rather than literally always abstaining), which does not weaken the exactness claim. Severity minor for that wording nit; the load-bearing claim itself survives intact and is confirmed both analytically and empirically.

verdicts.angle: τ GENERALIZATION — is the frozen margin threshold τ truly frozen one-shot before the sealed run, and does the sealed egDisagree==0 (bitwise-exactness on trusted commits) genuinely hold, or is it masked / vacuous / pinned by an outlier?
findings: FREEZE IS CRYPTOGRAPHICALLY CHAINED: frozen_tau_eg.json.inputSha256 (cce4e8f2...) recomputes EXACTLY from calib_eg_scatter.json's records. τ is provably derived from the CALIBRATION file (seeds [270000,270700)), not from the sealed file. chosenTau=95.93332505722272.

SEALED SET IS GENUINELY FRESH/DISJOINT: the offset-40 sealed slice (700 seeds) are HMAC-derived values 5.8M-2.1B, all >=FLOOR 290000, zero overlap with calib [270000,270700). The salt commitment (sha256_salt 2f2ee894...) was pre-registered in git on 2026-06-13 06:13, and the sealed report's commitment_sha256_salt matches it.

SEALED egDisagree==0 IS REAL AND NOT MASKED. I independently re-probed 180 sealed seeds in shadow mode (261,782 endgame commits, cell rotated per-seed, every N<=5 frame inspected) and found 0 trusted disagreements out of 22,701 GENUINE multi-boid (n>=2) trusted commits. maxTrustedMarginDisagree = -1 (none).

NOT VACUOUS VIA SOLE-REACHABLE n=1: in the sealed report 391/873 calib trusted are sole-reachable trivials, but the 482 genuine n>=2 calib trusted commits and the 22,701 genuine sealed trusted commits all agree. My probe found 1548 trusted commits in the razor band [tau, tau+5] (margin 95.94-100.9) ALL agree=true — the near-tau region is densely exercised, so 0-disagreement is not an artifact of only-huge-margin commits.

NO HIGHER-MARGIN DISAGREEMENT JUST PAST THE SAMPLE: across the 180 sealed seeds the HIGHEST disagreeing non-cert margin is 59.60 frames — a 36-frame gap below tau=95.93. disagreementsAtOrAboveTau = 0. The 'could a higher-margin disagreement exist just past the sealed sample' concern is empirically bounded well below tau.

RESIDUAL IS COMPUTED ON TRUSTED ONLY: residual_risk.trusted_commits=926 == trusted_total (NOT cert+trusted=8805). rule-of-three 3/926=0.00323974 reproduces exactly; per-game upper95 0.01008709 reproduces. cert (7879, 60.04%) is excluded as zero-risk — and that cert path is independently SOUND (0 false certs / 2,000,000 commits in eg_cert_soundness_2000000.json, eg_bound.js U<L proof with t<=TMAX guard).

GATE HAS TEETH: verdict_l1e.js line 156 process.exit((P.forceMismatch || P.egDisagree) ? 2 : 0) — a single sealed disagreement FAILS the verdict. All recomputed sealed totals (NN-share 0.671011, trusted-share 0.070569, cert-share 0.600442) match the report bit-for-bit; decomposition cert+trusted+fallback=13122=commits.

EXACTNESS-REDUCTION IS ARCHITECTURALLY VALID: l1e.js injects the gate as 'if(!egBoid && gate){...if(__egi>=0) egBoid=boids[__egi]}' immediately before prod's verbatim 'if(!egBoid){...}' commit block (predator_cheap.js:364). On abstain (-1) egBoid stays null and prod's argmin-scan + aim + steer (lines 364-385) run untouched, so fallback commits are bitwise-exact by construction. egBoid identity-agreement => force bitwise-identical holds.

MINOR FRAGILITY (the one real caveat): tau on CALIBRATION is pinned by a SINGLE outlier disagreement at margin 95.78; the next-highest calib disagreement is at 32.23 (a 63-frame jump). So the calibration-side safety gap below tau is only 0.157 frames and rests on one data point. The claim survives only because the FRESH sealed distribution shows a much wider 36-frame gap (worst=59.6) — generalization holds empirically, but the freeze procedure's calibration-side robustness is single-point-dependent.

SOFT NO-PEEKING CAVEAT: frozen_tau_eg.json, calib_eg_scatter.json and l1e_sealed_scatter.json all landed in the SAME git commit (f668f4b, 06-14 06:19), so git timestamps alone don't prove freeze-strictly-before-seal. Mitigated by the inputSha256 chain (tau derived only from calib) and the day-earlier (06-13) pre-registered salt commitment — peeking would require having computed tau from sealed data, which the sha256 chain rules out.
verdicts.reasoning: The exactness claim at the frozen τ survives this angle. I recomputed every load-bearing number from the raw artifacts (calib records, sealed report) and they match bit-for-bit, and I ran TWO independent shadow re-probes over the actual sealed seeds (261,782 commits) that the verdict harness did not surface. Those probes confirm the three things the angle demanded: (1) the sealed egDisagree==0 is genuine — 0 disagreements among 22,701 genuine n>=2 trusted commits, not masked by resync or by sole-reachable n=1 trivials (those are a separate 391-count bucket, all correct anyway); (2) the near-τ band is densely tested (1548 trusted commits within 5 frames of τ, all agree) so the zero is not vacuous; (3) no disagreement exists at or above τ on sealed — the empirical worst disagreement is 59.6 frames, 36 below τ, bounding the 'just past the sample' risk. The residual is correctly computed on the 926 trusted commits only (cert excluded and independently proven sound, 0/2M false certs), and the verdict exits non-zero on any disagreement, so the gate has real teeth. The freeze is cryptographically tied to the calib file via inputSha256 and the sealed seeds are provably fresh/disjoint. Severity is MINOR, not none, for two honest reasons: τ on the calibration side is pinned by a single outlier disagreement at 95.78 (0.16-frame gap, the rest of the calib disagreements sit below 32) so the calibration robustness is single-point-dependent; and the freeze + sealed verdict landed in one git commit, so timestamp-based no-peeking proof relies on the sha256 chain rather than ordering. Neither undermines bitwise-exactness — the sealed evidence is unambiguous and reproduces independently — but both are real limitations a careful auditor should record. The 67.1% NN-share (60.0% zero-risk cert + 7.1% τ-trusted) is accurate and the bitwise-exactness claim at the frozen τ is confirmed.

findings: n=1 TRIVIAL INFLATION (undisclosed in the headline): a sole boid (n=1) gets margin=Infinity from egboidPick AND a vacuously-true certificate (no competitor j), so EVERY n=1 commit lands in the NN-share numerator (cert if reachable, trusted if not) and NEVER in fallback (verified fallbackN1=0). These are trivially correct (one boid is always the argmin) and measure nothing about the NN on contested geometry.

8-seed SEALED spot check (10,188 commits, within the audit budget): n=1 = 25.1% of all commits, ALL in the numerator (1743 cert-n1 + 815 trusted-n1 = 2558 = exactly nTrivialTotal). Headline NN-share 62.2% drops to GENUINE non-trivial NN-share 49.5% (~13pp inflation); cert-share 45.4% -> 37.7%. Pooled estimate from the disclosed calib soleReachable (28.5%) gives the same ~13.1pp inflation on the published 67.1%.

DISCLOSURE GAP: candidates/l1e.js TRACKS soleN1/certNonTrivial/trustedNonTrivial (lines 99-134), but verdict_l1e.js reads only {commits,cert,trusted,fallback} (line 94/100) and never emits the trivial breakdown. The sealed report (l1e_sealed_scatter.json) and l1e_measurement_integrity.json publish ONLY the inflated 67.1% with no n=1 disclosure. The calib summary DID disclose soleReachable_n1=3765/13201 (28.5%), but that disclosure does not propagate to the sealed headline.

DISTRIBUTION REALISM (disclosed but headline-buried): the headline 67.1% NN-share / 60% cert is the SCATTER distribution (startBoids 2..5), NOT the deployed NATURAL full-game endgame. The team itself flags natural as lower (commit 138b6d8: ~41% cert / ~62% combined pooled; survivors are FAST, median speed 5.5 vs 1.0 -> harder scan-t -> fewer certifiable). The single in-flight natural cell (390x844, 76 egCommits) shows cert 34.2% / NN-share 48.7% vs scatter 58.6%/64.4% for the same cell, corroborating the gap. L1E_VERDICT.md line 56 calls natural 'the deployable one' with the natural row 'pending', so it IS disclosed in prose, but the single most-quoted number is the higher scatter one.

SOLID — no defect: per-cell egDisagree=0 AND forceMismatch=0 in ALL 6 cells of l1e_sealed_scatter.json (no pooled masking); harness egCommits==gate commits exactly in every cell (no double-count, perfect lockstep alignment); cert+trusted+fallback==commits and NN-share recomputes to 0.671010517 == published.

SOLID — seed integrity: sealing uses HMAC(secret salt) with FLOOR=290000, so sealed seeds are provably >=290000 and DISJOINT from calib [270000,280000) (verified min sealed seed, 0 overlap). Committed seal_commitment sha256 == live salt sha256 == sealed report's commitment_sha256_salt (2f2ee894...): no goalpost-moving. No global-state leak (global.__l1eStatsLast reset to null before each game at verdict_l1e.js:92; fresh stats object per create()).

SOLID — cross-check: harness egDisagree is measured against the REAL prod egBoid via the lockstep diff_harness (not eg_scan), and a tau=0/cert-off verdict makes NN-share=100% so egDisagree directly counts NN-vs-prod-egBoid mismatches; the calib shadow 'agree' uses eg_scan which is cross-engine bit-identical (V8 vs SpiderMonkey, 87,344 scores, 0 ulp diff). The cross-check claim (calib !agree count == harness egDisagree) is mechanically sound.

EXACTNESS IS DISTRIBUTION-INVARIANT (the safety claim is untouched): the fallback path returns -1 and runs prod's verbatim exact scan, absorbing every NN-uncertain case (band probe: 215 NN-alone-wrong cases all correctly routed to exact scan, 0 force impact; trustedDisagree=0 on sealed). So the inflation and distribution-realism issues affect ONLY the NN-SHARE marketing figure, never the bitwise-exactness/safety guarantee. egDisagree/forceMismatch = 0 on the natural cell so far.
verdicts.reasoning: The bitwise-exactness claim survives this angle completely: fallback makes exactness distribution-invariant, every cell shows 0 egDisagree/0 forceMismatch with no double-count or pooled masking, seed sealing is cryptographically disjoint from calibration, the salt commitment matches, there is no global-state leak, and the eg_scan cross-check is mechanically sound and cross-engine validated. However, the NN-SHARE HEADLINE does not survive as-presented. Two measurement issues inflate it: (1) ~25% of commits are n=1 trivials (sole boid, trivially correct) that all flow into the cert+trusted numerator, inflating the figure by ~13pp (genuine non-trivial NN-share is ~49.5%, not the headline 62-67%) — and although l1e.js computes the non-trivial breakdown, verdict_l1e.js silently drops it and the sealed evidence never discloses it. (2) The headline 67.1% is the scatter distribution, not the deployed natural distribution, which the team's own notes and the in-flight natural cell put materially lower (cert ~34-41%). The distribution gap is disclosed in prose (natural row 'pending', 'the deployable one'), but the n=1 trivial inflation is undisclosed in the sealed headline and is a real measurement-integrity defect. Severity is material (not fatal): the safety/exactness verdict stands; the corrective action is to publish certNonTrivial/trustedNonTrivial and the genuine non-trivial NN-share, and to make the NATURAL row the headline before sign-off rather than the inflated scatter number.

mustFix: MEASUREMENT (material, blocks sign-off as-presented). The NN-share HEADLINE does not survive. Root cause confirmed by code/artifact read: candidates/l1e.js DOES track the trivial breakdown (lines 100/130/132/134: soleN1, certNonTrivial, trustedNonTrivial), but verifier/verdict_l1e.js drops it — line 94 reads `gs = global.__l1eStatsLast || {commits,cert,trusted,fallback}` and line 100 only accumulates {commits,cert,trusted,fallback}; soleN1/certNonTrivial/trustedNonTrivial are never read or emitted. Consequently evidence/l1e_sealed_scatter.json and evidence/l1e_measurement_integrity.json publish only the inflated headline (NNshare 0.671010517, certShare 0.600442006) with ZERO n=1 disclosure. A sole boid (n=1) gets margin=Infinity AND a vacuously-true certificate, so every n=1 commit lands in the cert+trusted numerator and never in fallback — trivially correct (one boid is always the argmin) and measuring nothing about the NN on contested geometry. ANGLE 4's 8-seed spot check puts n=1 at ~25% of commits, inflating the 62-67% headline by ~13pp (genuine non-trivial NN-share ~49.5%). FIX: (1) extend verdict_l1e.js to read gs.soleN1/gs.certNonTrivial/gs.trustedNonTrivial and emit `NNshare_nonTrivial = (certNonTrivial+trustedNonTrivial)/(commits-soleN1)` plus the raw trivial counts in the report and per-cell; (2) re-run the SEALED scatter verdict to regenerate evidence/l1e_sealed_scatter.json + evidence/l1e_measurement_integrity.json with the breakdown; (3) restate L1E_VERDICT.md lines 88-92 and 103-104 to lead with the non-trivial NN-share, keeping 67.1% only as the trivials-included figure with the n=1 fraction shown alongside.

DISTRIBUTION (material, blocks sign-off as-presented). The single most-quoted number (67.1% NN-share, L1E_VERDICT.md line 88 and again line 103-104) is the SCATTER distribution (startBoids 2..5), but the file's own line 56 states 'The natural number is the deployable one' and the entire natural column (lines 83-92) is '_pending_'. The team's own notes (commit 138b6d8) and the in-flight natural cell put natural materially lower (cert ~34-41% vs scatter 60%) because natural-endgame survivors are faster (harder scan-t -> fewer certifiable). FIX: run `node verifier/verdict_l1e.js --natural --seeds <N> --sealOffset 40` to fill the natural row, then make the NATURAL non-trivial NN-share the headline figure for sign-off; demote scatter to a methodology/high-volume reference. The 'pending' verdict cannot be signed off until that row is measured. This is disclosed in prose but the load-bearing quoted number must be the deployable one.
synth.summary:
OVERALL L1e AUDIT VERDICT: the BITWISE-EXACTNESS / SAFETY claim SURVIVES; the NN-SHARE HEADLINE does NOT survive as-presented. Decision rule: 3 of 4 angles survive (cert-soundness: none; exactness-implication: minor; tau-generalization: minor), but ANGLE 4 (measurement-distribution) returned survives=false / severity=material. Per the synthesis rule, a single material finding means the verdict does not survive as-is.

WHAT IS ROCK-SOLID (verified against artifacts, not assumed):
- Force-exactness reduction is valid: intercept() downstream scan(egBoid)->aim->desired->steer (predator_cheap.js:372-385) is verbatim prod and branch-free on egBoid provenance; the gate is a proven pure-insert before the `if(!egBoid)` commit block, so a matching egBoid identity yields bitwise-identical force (ANGLE 2 confirmed analytically + empirically, forceMismatch=0, broken1ulp self-test catches a single-ulp divergence).
- Certificate is SOUND: U(k)<min L(j) sandwich (sound integer lower bound via triangle inequality + verified reachable upper bound + strict inequality), U<=TMAX guard closes the reachability-definition gap; evidence/eg_cert_soundness_2000000.json = 0 false certs / 2,000,000 (reproduced live, seed 3 -> certified 133154), plus ~146M fresh adversarial commits with 0 false certs (ANGLE 1, none).
- tau is one-shot frozen and chained: frozen_tau_eg.json.inputSha256 recomputes from the calibration file only; sealed seeds are HMAC-derived, FLOOR 290000, provably disjoint from calib [270000,270700); pre-registered salt commitment 2f2ee894 matches the sealed report. Sealed egDisagree==0 is genuine and not vacuous (22,701 genuine n>=2 trusted commits all agree; 1548 trusted commits within 5 frames of tau all agree; worst sealed disagreement 59.6f, a 36f gap below tau=95.93). verdict_l1e.js:156 exits non-zero on any egDisagree/forceMismatch, so the gate has teeth (ANGLE 3, minor: tau on the CALIBRATION side is pinned by one outlier at 95.78 with only a 0.16f gap; freeze+seal landed in one git commit so no-peeking rests on the sha256 chain not timestamps).
- Per-cell integrity: all 6 sealed cells show egDisagree=0 AND forceMismatch=0 (no pooled masking), harness egCommits==gate commits (13122==13122, no double-count), cert+trusted+fallback==commits, NNshare recomputes to 0.671010517 == published; seed sealing has no goalpost-moving and no global-state leak.

WHY THE HEADLINE FAILS (both material, both confirmed by direct read): (1) ~25% of commits are n=1 trivials that flow entirely into the cert+trusted numerator and never into fallback, inflating the headline ~13pp (genuine non-trivial NN-share ~49.5% vs published 67.1%). l1e.js computes the breakdown but verdict_l1e.js drops it and no published artifact discloses it. (2) The quoted 67.1% is the scatter distribution; the deployable natural distribution is '_pending_' and is materially lower (cert ~34-41%). See mustFix for exact files, lines, and corrective re-runs.

BOTTOM LINE: L0+L1h+L1e remain bitwise-exact to prod and the safety guarantee is distribution-invariant (fallback absorbs every NN-uncertain case; 215 NN-alone-wrong cases all routed to exact scan with 0 force impact). But the L1e SIGN-OFF cannot stand on the current headline: publish the non-trivial NN-share breakdown and lead with the NATURAL distribution before sign-off. No re-architecture, no exactness re-verification needed — this is a measurement-reporting fix plus the missing natural-distribution run.
